LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RACHEL ARCHULETA'S MOTION TO ENFORCE STIPULATED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 664.6, FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING PERFORMANCE AND FOR SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,996.00, AS AGAINST DEFENDANT ARYAN KENCHIN AND HIS ATTORNEYS OF RECORD SHEENA PATEL AND JOHN RANUCCI PER C.C.P. 128.5. PLAINTIFF RACHEL ARCHULETA'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. FOR CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 664.6 TO APPLY, THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT FOR SETTLEMENT OF THE CASE MUST BE "IN A WRITING SIGNED BY THE PARTIES OUTSIDE THE PRESENCE OF THE COURT OR ORALLY BEFORE THE COURT." (CODE CIV. PROC. 664.6.) THE TERM "PARTIES" MEANS THE LITIGANTS THEMSELVES, NOT THEIR ATTORNEYS OF RECORD. (LEVY V. SUPERIOR COURT (1995) 10 CAL.4TH 578, 586.) HOWEVER, WHEN THE DEFENSE IS BEING PROVIDED WITHOUT RESERVATION OF RIGHTS BY AN INSURER, AND IT HAS THE CONTRACTUAL RIGHT TO SETTLE WITHOUT THE INSURED'S CONSENT, THE SIGNATURE OR ORAL STIPULATION BY INSURANCE DEFENSE COUNSEL IS SUFFICIENT. (FIEGE V. COOKE (2004) 125 CAL.APP.4TH 1350, 1353-1355; ROBERTSON V. CHEN (1996) 44 CAL.APP.4TH 1290, 1293-96.) IN THIS CASE, NEITHER DEFENDANT ARYAN KENCHIN NOR THE INSURER SIGNED THE RELEASE AGREEMENT, AND THE RECORD INDICATES THAT THE INSURER WAS RESERVING ITS RIGHTS, SO THE EXCEPTION DOES NOT APPLY. (RANUCCI DECL., 3-4.) THE COURT DENIES BOTH PARTIES' REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement Agreement Pursuant To C.C.P. 664.6, For An Order Compelling Performance And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,996.00, As Against Defendant Aryan Kenchin And His Attorneys Of Record Sheena Patel And John Ranucci Per C.C.P. 128.5; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities; Decl. Of George M. Morris, Esq., In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar May-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RACHEL ARCHULETA's Motion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement Agreement Pursuant To C.C.P. 664.6, For An Order Compelling Performance And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,996.00, As Against Defendant Aryan Kenchin And His Attorneys Of Record Sheena Patel And John Ranucci Per C.C.P. 128.5.

Plaintiff Rachel Archuleta's motion to enforce settlement is denied. For Code of Civil Procedure 664.6 to apply, the parties' agreement for settlement of the case must be "in a writing signed by the parties outside the presence of the court or orally before the court." (Code Civ. Proc. 664.6.) The term "parties" means the litigants themselves, not their attorneys of record. (Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 586.) However, when the defense is being provided without reservation of rights by an insurer, and it has the contractual right to settle without the insured's consent, the signature or oral stipulation by insurance defense counsel is sufficient. (Fiege v. Cooke (2004) 125 Cal.App.4th 1350, 1353-1355; Robertson v. Chen (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1290, 1293-96.) In this case, neither Defendant Aryan Kenchin nor the insurer signed the release agreement, and the record indicates that the insurer was reserving its rights, so the exception does not apply. (Ranucci Decl., 3-4.) The court denies both parties' requests for sanctions.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Geroge M. Morris, Esq., Hassell Law Group, 415-334-4111, appearing for Plaintiff; (CourtCall)
Adam Isaac Miller, Miller Miller Gerber LLP, 714-450-3800 appearing for Defendant(s) Enterprise Rent a Car Co. of San Francisco; (CourtCall)

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RACHEL ARCHULETA'S Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Defendant Enterprise Rent-A-Car Company Of San Francisco, LLC.

Plaintiff Rachel Archuleta's motion to tax costs sought by Defendant Enterprise Rent-A-Car Company of San Francisco, LLC is denied. Plaintiff sued and served Enterprise as National Car Rental. On February 13, 2019, Defendants Enterprise and Enterprise Holdings, Inc. filed a demurrer to the complaint. On February 14, 2019, Plaintiff filed a request for dismissal without prejudice as to National Car Rental and Enterprise Holdings. As such, Enterprise (dba National) and Enterprise Holdings are prevailing parties entitled as a matter of right under Code of Civil Procedure section 1032(b) to recover costs. (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 1032(a)(4) [prevailing party includes a defendant in whose favor a dismissal is entered]; Mon Chong Loong Trading Corp. v. Superior Court (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 87, 94 ["While a lawsuit may be concluded by a voluntary dismissal, the price of such a dismissal is the payment of costs under section 1032," even if potential exists that plaintiff may refile the same action].) Plaintiff's reliance on cases involving statutory fee-shifting provisions is misplaced, as the definition of prevailing party for purposes of those provisions differs from that under the cost statute. (E.g., Salehi v. Surfside III Condominium Owners Ass'n (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1146, 1153.)

Order is signed.
